question.text: What is the maximum limit for purchase of equity shares of a single company by a foreign portfolio investor including its investor group?

answer.text: Below 15 percent of the total paid-up equity capital on a fully diluted basis
Below 25 percent of the total paid-up equity capital on a fully diluted basis
Below 5 percent of the total paid-up equity capital on a fully diluted basis
Below 10 percent of the total paid-up equity capital on a fully diluted basis

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per Regulation 20(6) of SEBI (FPI) Regulations, 2019, the purchase of equity shares of each company by a single FPI including its investor group shall be below 10 percent of the total paid-up equity capital on a fully diluted basis of the company. **The Trap:** Candidates may confuse this 10% single FPI limit with the NRI individual limit of 5% under PIS, or the aggregate FPI limit of 24%. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If an FPI breaches the 10% threshold, it must divest excess holdings within 5 trading days. Failure to divest means the entire investment becomes FDI.
